judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In the execute_python step, the agent extracted publication year by taking the first 4-digit year found anywhere in the paper text (years[0]), which often captured an earlier reference year rather than the actual publication year, causing the post-2016 filter to exclude all papers and yield an empty result despite valid matches (trace: call_hTXGR70FFfldDd7sbwMoVHW9).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After querying the paper texts and citation totals, the agent’s execute_python step (call_MsT2oqZKgqpVm4hMLAHkEOxh) used naive heuristics—searching for the literal word "empirical" anywhere in the text and taking the first year in the first 2000 characters—to infer contribution and publication year, which led to no matches and the erroneous "No papers found" result despite the correct data being available.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
In execute_python, the agent filtered papers by checking if the full text literally contained the word "empirical" and set publication year as the first 4-digit number found in the text, causing valid post-2016 empirical papers to be excluded and yielding an empty result despite correct data selection and aggregation.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes

The agent loaded paper texts and citation totals, then in execute_python labeled 'empirical' papers by a naive substring search for the word "empirical" in full text and filtered by the first 4-digit year > 2016, which resulted in an empty set and the final "No papers found" answer despite relevant papers existing.
